notes: This manifest defines the initial committed public cases for the MathDevMCP real-task benchmark program.
Executable loading, validation, and report integration are deferred to a later implementation slice.
Paths are stored as repo-root-relative paths. Sibling repositories may be referenced via ../ segments from the MathDevMCP checkout root.

cases.prompt: Audit the large-scale LGSSM missing-data policy and determine what behavior is intentionally unsupported, which failures are explicit pending-policy failures, and which are plain configuration errors. Do not promote these checks into posterior or model-validity claims.

required_terms: Masked Kalman updates and derivative slicing are pending
forbidden_claims: This validates Kalman likelihood correctness under missing data.
This establishes posterior validity for sparse panels.
required_next_actions: Treat sparse or masked observations as unsupported until masked updates are implemented.
Keep NaN sparse proxies out of the likelihood path.

cases.notes: Deterministic explicit-failure policy case with clear pending/error semantics.

cases.prompt: Summarize what the HMC regression helper tests establish and what they explicitly do not establish. Preserve the distinction between numerical-stability helper evidence and model-level identification or posterior evidence.

forbidden_claims: These tests establish structural identification.
These tests validate the posterior.
These tests prove sampler convergence for the model.
required_next_actions: Use separate model-level diagnostics for identification or posterior claims.

cases.notes: Boundary-discipline case anchored by explicit comments in the test file itself.

cases.prompt: Read the strict NK convergence audit and extract the exact pass criteria, run scope, and non-smoke interpretation. Preserve the fact that this is a heavyweight statistical audit rather than a routine local smoke test.

forbidden_claims: This is a quick smoke test.
The file contains no explicit convergence thresholds.
required_next_actions: Use the declared thresholds as the audit contract for this case.

cases.notes: Quantitative benchmark case with explicit thresholds and scope boundaries.

cases.prompt: Audit the BayesFilter QR Kalman migration parity test and determine exactly what is certified by the tiny fixture. Report the direct-value parity target and the explicit capability boundaries around score authority and XLA HMC readiness.

forbidden_claims: This certifies BayesFilter score authority.
This proves XLA HMC readiness.
This proves full filter gradient certification.
required_next_actions: Treat this as tiny-fixture value-parity evidence only.
Keep score-authority and XLA-readiness claims separate.

cases.notes: Strong parity case because the test file encodes both exact equality and explicit capability limits.

cases.prompt: Summarize the BayesFilter model-suite HMC qualification result and state precisely what kind of evidence it provides, along with the stronger conclusions the note explicitly says are not justified.

forbidden_claims: This result proves convergence.
This result establishes posterior validity.
This result authorizes scientific interpretation.
required_next_actions: Require a serious DSGE/NK experiment plan before stronger claims are made.

cases.notes: High-value evidence-boundary case because the source note already states explicit non-claims.

cases.prompt: Find the analytical validation material for the linear-Gaussian state-space model and extract the exact setup, the EDH/Kalman exactness claim, and the practical test specification including the concrete pass criterion.

forbidden_claims: The chapter provides no concrete pass criterion.
The chapter only gives qualitative validation advice.
required_next_actions: Use the practical test specification as a gold-standard benchmark case.

cases.notes: Strong analytical gold case with explicit proposition-level structure and a practical validation threshold.

cases.prompt: Audit the basis-reconciliation plan and recover the relationship between the market basis and the model basis, including the multi-location sign-convention risk. Preserve uncertainty about anything the plan flags as still requiring audit or chapter harmonization.

forbidden_claims: The monograph is already fully reconciled.
No further chapter-level harmonization is needed.
required_next_actions: Treat the document as a reconciliation task rather than a settled single-source definition.
Audit downstream chapter references against the proposed master reconciliation section.

cases.notes: Good abstention-quality case because the source is a plan diagnosing unresolved multi-location sign drift.

cases.prompt: Summarize the short HMC acceptance-veto diagnosis result and preserve the fact that trusted same-process GPU provenance failed before any reviewed rows could be interpreted. Do not treat this as pilot closure or downstream authorization.

required_terms: acceptance-veto diagnosis requires trusted same-process GPU execution
forbidden_claims: short HMC pilot gate closure: authorized
synthetic or empirical HMC pilot: authorized
overnight or full estimation launch: authorized
required_next_actions: fix the diagnostic blocker or revise under Claude/Codex review

cases.notes: Public inconclusive case with explicit blocker, veto diagnostics, and downstream non-authorization language.

cases.prompt: Summarize the SGU filter-target readiness result while preserving the explicit blocked nonlinear manifold residual. Do not upgrade local engineering readiness into exact nonlinear manifold closure or downstream Bayesian/HMC readiness.

forbidden_claims: exact nonlinear manifold closure is achieved
BayesFilter integration is certified
posterior readiness is established
required_next_actions: Preserve the nonlinear manifold blocker as open in downstream planning.

cases.notes: Public mismatch case where a local readiness result explicitly preserves a blocker that must not be erased by later summaries.

cases.prompt: Audit the DenseSOAP source-contract result and preserve the fact that both local TensorFlow surfaces remain diagnostic-only because of material official-contract mismatches. Do not promote them to parity or default optimizer policy.

forbidden_claims: official SOAP parity is established
default optimizer policy should be promoted
downstream DSGE/HMC readiness is established
required_next_actions: Keep both TensorFlow DenseSOAP surfaces diagnostic-only unless a new bounded optimizer plan is approved.

cases.notes: Public mismatch case with explicit static source-contract drift and strong nonclaims against parity promotion.

cases.prompt: Summarize the NeuTra real-NK BayesFilter migration certification result while preserving the fail-closed engineering gate: the current runner is not migrated and serious real-NK NeuTra continuation remains blocked.

forbidden_claims: the current DSGE real-NK NeuTra runner is migrated
serious real-NK NeuTra baselines are ready
score-matching continuation is ready
required_next_actions: Implement the remaining BayesFilter migration checklist before serious real-NK NeuTra continuation.

cases.notes: Public mismatch case with explicit fail-closed migration blockers and strong nonclaims against readiness promotion.

cases.prompt: Audit the ResearchAssistant benchmark inventory discipline and summarize what benchmark artifacts and report fields are required for its parser benchmark workflow. Focus on inventory structure rather than parser correctness claims.

forbidden_claims: This test suite proves parser semantic correctness beyond its declared scored fields.
required_next_actions: Reuse its benchmark-inventory discipline as a structural reference, not as a semantic oracle for MathDevMCP outputs.

cases.notes: Useful adjacent benchmark-pattern case for manifest/report structure discipline.
